text.title: Task Creation and Parallelization Analysis
text.summary: Workflow for generating TASK_NNN.md files from an approved implementation plan and analyzing parallelization via wave detection.

list.item: Step 1: Verify spec.md and design.md both have status Approved
Step 2: Verify implementation-plan.md exists and is Approved
Step 3: For each task row in the plan — call faion-task-creator-agent with task info + SDD path
Step 4: Run faion-sdd-reviewer-agent (mode: tasks) — 4-pass review (completeness, consistency, coverage, executability)
Step 5: Move approved tasks to todo/ folder

rule.statement: Pass 4 (Executability) is the critical review pass: can each task run standalone within 100k tokens without reading the full codebase?
rule.rationale: Tasks that fail the executability check produce executor agents that stall mid-execution trying to discover missing context. Executability failures are cheaper to fix in the planning phase than during execution.

example.note: Planning subagent prompt: "PROJECT: {project}. FEATURE: {feature}. TASK_INFO: {task row from implementation-plan.md}. SDD_PATH: .aidocs/features/{status}/{feature}/. Read dependency task summaries. Create TASK_{NNN}.md following the v2.0 template. Token estimate must be <100k. AC in Given-When-Then. Include full dependency tree."

4-pass reviewer prompt: "MODE: tasks. Review all TASK_*.md files for feature {feature}. Pass 1: completeness — all plan rows covered? Pass 2: consistency — no contradictions between tasks? Pass 3: coverage — every FR and AD referenced? Pass 4: executability — can each task run standalone within 100k tokens?"

section.title: Parallelization Analysis Workflow

list.item: Step 1: Read all TASK_*.md files in the feature
Step 2: Extract explicit dependencies from each task's Dependencies section
Step 3: Detect implicit dependencies — use find-shared-files.py for file-level conflicts
Step 5: Group into waves (independent tasks together)
Step 6: Add checkpoints between waves (merge, verify, review, sync)
Step 7: Calculate theoretical speedup: sequential / max-path-per-wave

rule.statement: Real parallelization speedup is 30-50% of theoretical due to coordination overhead; do not commit to a speedup estimate in planning documents.
rule.rationale: I/O-bound tasks, merge conflicts, and checkpoint overhead consume the theoretical speedup. Overpromising on speedup creates false timeline expectations.

section.reference: Detect files touched by multiple tasks — creates implicit FS dependencies
